title: Seven injured as tree falls on football field in Shizuoka
summary: The collapse of a 20-meter tree during a match injured seven people, including children, who were taken to hospital.

title: Argentina and Spain contest 2026 World Cup final in historic clash
summary: Great anticipation for the decisive match includes extremely expensive tickets, a halftime show with BTS and Madonna, and a record global audience.

title: Knife attack in Osaka: three women injured and teenage attacker dead
summary: An 18-year-old youth broke into an apartment and stabbed a mother and her two daughters before falling from a height and dying.

title: Sasaki Rintaro chooses Miami Marlins to start his MLB career
summary: The Japanese pitcher rejects the local draft to try his luck in the United States, receiving support from his former team and other players.

title: Floods and landslides in northern Kanto leave at least two dead
summary: Torrential rains caused house collapses in Ashikaga and the collapse of a bridge in Maebashi, with fatal victims.

title: Nagoya sumo tournament reaches its midpoint with four undefeated leaders
summary: After eight days, four wrestlers share the first place with only one loss, including Daieisho and Hōshōryu.

title: Japan provides security assistance to Cambodia through new OSA mechanism
summary: The Japanese government supplies communication equipment and patrol boats to strengthen maritime security cooperation.

title: Man falls from apartment and young woman dies after being hit in Nagoya
summary: The 23-year-old victim was walking on the sidewalk when the man jumped from a 12th floor; he was seriously injured.

title: Olympic medalist Toshiaki Fushimi dies in keirin accident
summary: The former cyclist, silver medalist in Athens 2004, suffered a fatal fall during a race in Matsusaka.

title: Russia launches largest missile attack on Kiev while Ukraine strikes Russian facilities
summary: More than 40 missiles hit the Ukrainian capital, while Ukrainian drones struck oil depots and logistics centers in Russia.

title: Approval of Takaichi's cabinet falls to 41% and disapproval surpasses it for first time
summary: Mainichi survey shows decline in support for Japanese government, which faces growing criticism.

title: Japan faces extreme heat wave with temperatures up to 38 degrees
summary: Authorities recommend measures against heatstroke as high temperatures are forecast in wide areas.

title: Japan dominates preliminary phase of Men's Volleyball Nations League with 12 victories
summary: The Japanese team completes the first round without losing a single match, a historic achievement.

title: President of Hungary resigns after signing constitutional amendment
summary: The president, accused of being a puppet of Orbán, sees his term shortened amid opposition criticism.

title: Fire on fishing boat off Miura: all 11 occupants saved and boat sinks
summary: All passengers and crew were evacuated safe and sound before the vessel caught fire and sank.

title: Yamaguchi Akane finishes runner-up at Japan Open and prepares for Asian Games
summary: The Japanese badminton player shows her competitive level in both consecutive international tournaments.

title: Fire at entertainment center in Bangkok leaves 34 dead
summary: Thai authorities order emergency inspections at all entertainment venues in the country after the incident.

title: United States launches strikes for eight consecutive days after death of two soldiers
summary: Iran announces suspension of bilateral memorandum amid escalating tensions in the Middle East.

title: Burnham takes office as new UK Prime Minister, the seventh in ten years
summary: The Labour politician promises to promote devolution to revitalize British government.

title: ICC President defends court against Trump's threats of dismantlement
summary: Tomoko Akane states that the International Criminal Court will continue its work despite political challenges.
